explanation: This command includes key command-line arguments from the Sigma rule (find, /vulnerable, /template, /altname, /domain) and uses the expected executable name. It simulates a typical Certify usage pattern without obfuscation.

This command uses the 'request' subcommand and includes the /ca, /path, and /template options, which are part of the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It represents a typical Certify command line for certificate requests.
This command uses the 'download' subcommand and includes the /path and /ca options, aligning with the Sigma rule's detection logic. It reflects a common usage pattern for downloading certificates.

This command uses the 'pkiobjects' subcommand with the /domain and /vulnerable flags, which are part of the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It represents a typical Certify command line for enumerating PKI objects.
